{"step_by_step_thinking": "Clonidine is an antihypertensive drug that acts by inhibiting sympathetic outflow and reducing norepinephrine release. It is not directly involved in lowering intraocular pressure (IOP). Mannitol is an osmotic diuretic that decreases the volume of aqueous humor and lowers IOP. Dexamethasone is a corticosteroid that can increase IOP as a side effect. Acetazolamide is a carbonic anhydrase inhibitor that reduces aqueous humor production and lowers IOP.", "answer_choice": "C"}
